The package has 51 generated files covering clusters, servers, configurations, firewall rules, coordinator/node configurations, private endpoint connections, and roles — but the README remains a generic boilerplate with no service-specific content.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **No service-specific key concepts**: The \"Key concepts\" section links only to a generic Azure SDK page; it does not describe CosmosDB for PostgreSQL resources (Cluster, Server, FirewallRule, Configuration, CoordinatorConfiguration, NodeConfiguration, PrivateEndpointConnection, Role)\n- **No code examples in README**: The \"Examples\" section links only to a generic Azure SDK samples page; no inline code snippets showing how to create a cluster, list servers, manage firewall rules, or update configurations\n- **No test-backed snippets**: There is no `tests/Samples/` directory and no `#region Snippet:` tags anywhere in the package — the snippet tooling (`Update-Snippets.ps1`) cannot inject any examples\n- **CHANGELOG 1.1.0-beta.3 is empty**: The upcoming unreleased version section has blank `Features Added`, `Breaking Changes`, `Bugs Fixed`, and `Other Changes` entries even though the TypeSpec migration may have introduced API surface changes\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n#### File: `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/README.md`\n\n**Edit** the README to replace the generic sections with service-specific content.\n\nReplace the generic \"Key concepts\" section with:\n\n```markdown\n## Key concepts\n\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql` library provides resource management for [Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cosmos-db/postgresql/introduction), a managed service for running PostgreSQL workloads at scale.\n\n### Resource hierarchy\n\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlCluster** — The top-level resource representing a cluster (a group of nodes). Create, update, restart, or delete a cluster and promote replicas to independent read-write clusters.\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterServer** — Represents an individual server (coordinator or worker) within a cluster.\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRule** — Manages firewall rules that control external access to the cluster.\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlConfiguration** — A cluster-level configuration parameter applying to all nodes.\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlCoordinatorConfiguration** — A configuration parameter scoped to the coordinator node.\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlNodeConfiguration** — A configuration parameter scoped to worker nodes.\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlPrivateEndpointConnection** — Manages private endpoint connections for the cluster.\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlRole** — Manages database roles (users) within the cluster.\n```\n\nReplace the generic \"Examples\" section with:\n\n```markdown\n## Examples\n\nThe following sections show code samples for common management scenarios.\n\n### Create a cluster\n\n```C# Snippet:CreateCosmosDBForPostgreSqlCluster\n```\n\n### List all clusters in a subscription\n\n```C# Snippet:ListCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusters\n```\n\n### Create a firewall rule\n\n```C# Snippet:CreateCosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRule\n```\n\n### Update a configuration parameter\n\n```C# Snippet:UpdateCosmosDBForPostgreSqlConfiguration\n```\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nCreate the file `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/tests/Samples/Sample1_Clusters.cs` with NUnit test methods that wrap sample code in `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` tags.\n\n**File to create:** `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/tests/Samples/Sample1_Clusters.cs`\n\n```csharp\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\n\nusing System;\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\nusing Azure.Identity;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql.Models;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\nusing NUnit.Framework;\n\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql.Tests.Samples\n{\n    public class Sample1_Clusters : SamplesBase\u003cCosmosDBForPostgreSqlManagementTestEnvironment\u003e\n    {\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample builds\")]\n        public async Task CreateCluster()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:CreateCosmosDBForPostgreSqlCluster\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n\n            // Retrieve the subscription and resource group\n            string subscriptionId = \"00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\";\n            string resourceGroupName = \"myResourceGroup\";\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\"))\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName);\n\n            // Prepare cluster create parameters\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterData data = new CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterData(\n                new Azure.Core.AzureLocation(\"eastus\"))\n            {\n                PostgresqlVersion = \"15\",\n                AdministratorLoginPassword = \"myP@ssword1\",\n                CoordinatorStorageQuotaInMb = 524288,\n                CoordinatorVCores = 4,\n                NodeCount = 2,\n                NodeStorageQuotaInMb = 524288,\n                NodeVCores = 4,\n            };\n\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterCollection clusters = resourceGroup.GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusters();\n            ArmOperation\u003cCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource\u003e operation = await clusters\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \"myCluster\", data);\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource cluster = operation.Value;\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Cluster provisioning state: {cluster.Data.ProvisioningState}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample builds\")]\n        public async Task ListClusters()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:ListCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusters\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n            string subscriptionId = \"00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\";\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\"))\n                .GetAsync();\n\n            await foreach (CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource cluster in subscription\n                .GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClustersAsync())\n            {\n                Console.WriteLine(cluster.Data.Name);\n            }\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample builds\")]\n        public async Task CreateFirewallRule()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:CreateCosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRule\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n            string subscriptionId = \"00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\";\n            string resourceGroupName = \"myResourceGroup\";\n            string clusterName = \"myCluster\";\n\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\"))\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName);\n\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource cluster = await resourceGroup\n                .GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterAsync(clusterName);\n\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRuleData ruleData =\n                new CosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRuleData(\"0.0.0.0\", \"255.255.255.255\");\n\n            ArmOperation\u003cCosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRuleResource\u003e operation = await cluster\n                .GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRules()\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \"allowAll\", ruleData);\n\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Firewall rule provisioning state: {operation.Value.Data.ProvisioningState}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample builds\")]\n        public async Task UpdateConfiguration()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:UpdateCosmosDBForPostgreSqlConfiguration\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n            string subscriptionId = \"00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\";\n            string resourceGroupName = \"myResourceGroup\";\n            string clusterName = \"myCluster\";\n            string configurationName = \"array_nulls\";\n\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\"))\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName);\n\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource cluster = await resourceGroup\n                .GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterAsync(clusterName);\n\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlConfigurationResource configuration = await cluster\n                .GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlConfigurationAsync(configurationName);\n\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Configuration '{configurationName}' current value: {configuration.Data.Value}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Snippet names and their README placeholder locations:**\n\n| Snippet name | README section | Placeholder to add |\n|---|---|---|\n| 2026-04-07T05:42:57.7113082Z **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql`
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7113610Z **Service directory:** `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/`
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7114126Z **Triggered by:** commit e373db219 (#57114) by @haiyuan_zhang
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7114354Z 
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7114430Z ## What Changed
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7114545Z 
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7116091Z The MPG migration for CosmosDB for PostgreSQL was completed, adding `ci.mgmt.yml` and `test-resources.json` to establish the TypeSpec-based CI pipeline. The package has 51 generated files covering clusters, servers, configurations, firewall rules, coordinator/node configurations, private endpoint connections, and roles — but the README remains a generic boilerplate with no service-specific content.
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7117487Z 
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7117567Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7117677Z 
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7118585Z - **No service-specific key concepts**: The "Key concepts" section links only to a generic Azure SDK page; it does not describe CosmosDB for PostgreSQL resources (Cluster, Server, FirewallRule, Configuration, CoordinatorConfiguration, NodeConfiguration, PrivateEndpointConnection, Role)
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7120489Z - **No code examples in README**: The "Examples" section links only to a generic Azure SDK samples page; no inline code snippets showing how to create a cluster, list servers, manage firewall rules, or update configurations
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7121927Z - **No test-backed snippets**: There is no `tests/Samples/` directory and no `#region Snippet:` tags anywhere in the package — the snippet tooling (`Update-Snippets.ps1`) cannot inject any examples
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7123294Z - **CHANGELOG 1.1.0-beta.3 is empty**: The upcoming unreleased version section has blank `Features Added`, `Breaking Changes`, `Bugs Fixed`, and `Other Changes` entries even though the TypeSpec migration may have introduced API surface changes
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7124096Z 
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7124165Z <details>
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7124469Z <summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7124826Z 
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7125136Z This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7125538Z 
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7125619Z ### Step 1: Modify files
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7125755Z 
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7126034Z #### File: `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/README.md`
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7126397Z 
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7126604Z **Edit** the README to replace the generic sections with service-specific content.
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7126899Z 
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7127020Z Replace the generic "Key concepts" section with:
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7127218Z 
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7127290Z ```markdown
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7127458Z ## Key concepts
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7127565Z 
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7128850Z The `Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql` library provides resource management for [Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cosmos-db/postgresql/introduction), a managed service for running PostgreSQL workloads at scale.
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7128875Z 
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7129193Z ### Resource hierarchy
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7129201Z 
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7130402Z - **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlCluster** — The top-level resource representing a cluster (a group of nodes). Create, update, restart, or delete a cluster and promote replicas to independent read-write clusters.
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7131078Z - **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterServer** — Represents an individual server (coordinator or worker) within a cluster.
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7131764Z - **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRule** — Manages firewall rules that control external access to the cluster.
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7132422Z - **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlConfiguration** — A cluster-level configuration parameter applying to all nodes.
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7133109Z - **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlCoordinatorConfiguration** — A configuration parameter scoped to the coordinator node.
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7133747Z - **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlNodeConfiguration** — A configuration parameter scoped to worker nodes.
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7134482Z - **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlPrivateEndpointConnection** — Manages private endpoint connections for the cluster.
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7134996Z - **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlRole** — Manages database roles (users) within the cluster.
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7135142Z ```
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7135149Z 
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7135271Z Replace the generic "Examples" section with:
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7135276Z 
2026-04-07T05:42:57.7135353Z ```markdown
